visual_features.overall_style: Clean, modern, and professional with a calm, child-appropriate tone. The look is more therapeutic and reassuring than overtly playful: lots of solid color fields, simple typography, and a single strong illustrative element (ink-cloud) rather than busy graphics.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is established through strong size contrast and central placement. The logo anchors the top-left, with a horizontal nav in the upper center/right. Below, a large full-width hero band with a solid slate-blue background and a central dark ink-cloud shape creates a strong focal area. The hero title 'CLOUDKID & THE MOUNTAIN' is the dominant visual element due to its size, high contrast (white outline type on dark blob), and central alignment. Secondary hierarchy: the nav links (uppercase teal text) and then smaller heading text below the hero (e.g., 'YOUR SPACE THEATRE').
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous whitespace overall. The header has ample vertical padding above and below the logo and nav, giving a light, breathable feel. The hero area is very spacious: large empty slate-blue space around the ink-cloud illustration and centered headline. There is no visible clutter, and elements are separated by comfortable margins.
unique_elements: Organic ink-cloud / watercolor blot hero shape that frames the main title
Handwritten/script-style logo for 'Your Space' combined with a more clinical sans-serif for 'THERAPIES'
All-caps, widely letter-spaced hero headline overlaying an irregular organic shape
Top nav item with distinct color treatment (TRAINING) to emphasize a key area

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column desktop grid with a centered max-width content container (~1100–1200px) inside full-width color bands.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic header + full-width hero layout. The header is a horizontal bar with left-aligned logo and right/centered nav; below it, a full-width hero section with centered content.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: From what is visible: a fixed-height (or auto-height) header followed by a full-width hero band. Sections likely stack vertically in a single-column flow below the hero, alternating background colors (white vs slate blue) for contrast.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Left alignment for brand/logo, center alignment for navigation items across the top. Hero content is center-centered (both horizontally and vertically within the hero band). Text within the hero is horizontally centered with generous letter-spacing.
responsive_hints: Nav items are widely spaced with enough room to collapse into a mobile menu at smaller widths.
Hero title is centrally placed and likely scales down/ wraps across lines gracefully on smaller screens.
Full-width hero band suggests background-color blocks that adapt easily to responsive layouts.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: (1) top header with logo on the left and a single-row horizontal navigation in the center/right, all on a white background; (2) immediately below, a full-width hero section with a solid slate-blue background; in the hero, an organic dark ink-cloud shape is horizontally centered, and the main title text is centered inside the shape on two lines. Below the hero title (just visible) is another centered all-caps heading ('YOUR SPACE THEATRE'), likely starting the next content section.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Within the initial viewport, roughly 15–20% height is occupied by the header (logo + nav) and 80–85% by the hero band. The hero allocates a large central area to the ink-cloud and title, with generous negative space above, below, and on the sides, reinforcing focus on the title. The logo block in the header takes about one-third of the header width (left), and the nav uses the central/right two-thirds.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious layout with ample breathing room between header and hero and large margins around the central graphic. Information density is low: few elements per section and plenty of padding, which suits the calm, therapeutic context and ensures easy scanning for parents and educators.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Strong vertical flow. The eye starts at the logo in the top-left, then is pulled rightwards across the navigation labels, then moves down to the large, centered ink-cloud and bold hero title. Because the hero content is centered and isolated, the user’s attention is held there before continuing to the text below (e.g., 'YOUR SPACE THEATRE'). The pattern is close to a simple F-pattern on the header followed by a strong central focal point in the hero.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons are not explicitly visible in the screenshot, but based on the flat, modern aesthetic and nav text styling, a consistent button style would be a rounded rectangle with 4–6px corner radius, solid fill using the primary teal (#00A0A8) or accent gold (#C0B46A), and white uppercase text. Padding likely around 12–16px vertically and 24–32px horizontally. No heavy gradients; flat or very subtle shadow (or none) to keep a clean look. Hover state would probably invert color (white background, teal border and text) or darken the fill slightly.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Semantically correct <button> elements for primary actions, and <a> elements styled as buttons for navigational CTAs (e.g., 'Learn more', 'Contact us'). Buttons inside flexbox containers or centered block wrappers for alignment within sections.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Cards are not visible but the brand suggests soft, approachable containers: white or very light neutral (#F5F7FA) backgrounds on top of colored or white sections, with a thin 1px border in a light gray or a very subtle soft shadow (e.g., 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)). Corners likely slightly rounded (4–8px). Internally, cards would use 16–24px padding with clear separation between title, descriptive text, and any actions. Headlines may be all caps or sentence-case sans-serif, with clear spacing below.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Div-based cards, each as a flex or block container: <article> or <div class='card'> containing an optional icon/image at top, a heading (<h3>), supporting paragraph text, and CTA link/button. Multiple cards would likely be arranged in a responsive grid (2–3 per row on desktop, 1 per row on mobile) using CSS grid or flexbox.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No form fields are visible, but expected styling would align with the clean, calm aesthetic: full-width text inputs with 4px radius, 1px light-gray border, and good vertical padding (8–12px). Labels likely above each field for clarity. Focus state would use a teal border and subtle glow or border-color change. Error states might show a red border with a small error message in red beneath the field. Overall forms would be medium-density: not crowded, with sufficient vertical spacing (16–24px) between fields.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Stacked labels above inputs within a vertical form layout: <form> containing <div class='form-field'> wrappers, each with <label> followed by <input> or <textarea>. Grouped logical sections possibly wrapped in <fieldset> with a <legend> for longer forms (e.g., referral or contact forms). Submit buttons at the end, aligned left or centered depending on context.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Horizontal top navigation with uppercase sans-serif menu items spaced evenly across the header width. Text color appears teal (#00A0A8) on a white background. One menu item ('TRAINING') uses a different color (muted gold/olive, approx. #C0B46A) to highlight it as a key area. There is significant horizontal padding between items, contributing to the airy feel. Active/hover states are likely indicated by color changes (e.g., darker teal or underlines).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: A semantic <nav> element containing an unordered list <ul> of <li> items, each with an <a> link. CSS flexbox used to distribute items horizontally. Dropdowns (as suggested by small ▼/caret on items like 'ABOUT US' and 'TRAINING') would be implemented as nested <ul> lists toggled via hover/focus on desktop and click/tap on mobile.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Lists are not visible in the screenshot, but likely to be simple, low-density content lists: bullet or icon-led items with moderate spacing (8–12px) between items. For more visual content (e.g., services or programs), lists might present as card-like rows with an icon or small image on the left and text on the right, maintaining ample whitespace.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
For textual lists: standard
    or
      with custom bullet styles (possibly small teal circles). For richer items: 'media object' pattern using flexbox, with
    1. or
      containing an or icon span followed by a text container

heading.visual_style: All-caps, geometric sans-serif with clean lines and fairly large letter-spacing, conveying clarity and modernity. The hero type looks structured and bold but not overly heavy, matching a friendly yet professional tone.

body.visual_style: Readable, neutral sans-serif suited for paragraphs and explanatory content, likely with slightly rounded forms to feel friendly and approachable.

spacing_system.component_padding: Most interactive components (buttons, cards, inputs) should use 16px–24px internal padding (2–3 base units) for comfortable touch/click targets and a spacious feel.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections should be 64px–96px (8–12 base units), matching the generous breathing room seen between the header and the hero and supporting a calm, non-cluttered reading experience.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change color on hover (darker teal or underline) and maintain a distinct accent color for the current/important section (e.g., 'TRAINING').
Buttons should have clear hover states: slight darkening or lightening of background color, and possibly a subtle shadow or scale-up transition (e.g., transform: scale(1.02)).
Hero elements (like the ink-cloud or title) could have gentle fade-in or slide-up animations on load or scroll to reinforce a sense of storytelling without overstimulation.
Dropdown menus (for ABOUT US and TRAINING) should use smooth opacity/translate transitions (150–200ms) to feel responsive yet calm.
Links in body text can use color change and subtle underline-on-hover transitions to signal interactivity while staying consistent with the minimal aesthetic.
